Items.Title: Postman 替代方案：Hoppscotch 🛸 - 开源、自托管 API 开发生态系统 | Postman Alternative: Hoppscotch 🛸 - open source, self hosted API Development Ecosystem
Items.Description:
在过去的一周里，我们观察到越来越多的人寻求 Postman 的替代品，因为他们渴望有更合适的选择。​


​

在Hoppscotch 🛸，我们正在构建一个 API 开发生态系统，其核心重点是提供最佳的开发人员体验。 在本博客中，我们将探讨Hoppscotch的功能，并解释它如何增强您的 API 构建和测试体验。

开源👥

随着时间的推移建立的透明度和信任使得我们能够迅速解决问题，就功能请求进行公开讨论，并提供一个稳定可靠的平台。

收藏📂

顾名思义，集合是一组有组织的 API 请求。 使用 Hoppscotch，您可以创建任意数量的集合，并在文件夹内组织保存的请求，以便更好地导航。

多协议支持➕

执行REST API请求、 GraphQL 查询并使用 Web Socket、Socket.IO、SSE 和 MQTT 等实时协议。

环境⚙️

环境是一组可重用的键值对，可用于创建请求和编写脚本。 它还有助于管理工作区中团队成员之间的共享数据。 在访问请求 URL 栏中的环境变量时，Hoppscotch 提供了一个编辑属性，可以根据需要随时更改变量。

测试和脚本支持🛠

创建测试用例并对发送请求后收到的响应执行测试运行。 使用侧边栏上的片段来了解不同字段的更多信息。

历史🕜

使用 Hoppscotch 跟踪您的 API 请求和响应。 一旦您点击“发送”发起请求，hoppscotch 就会记录该呼叫并保存在“历史记录”下。 要查看过去的请求，请单击“历史记录”图标并在需要时进行访问。

团队协作🤝

使用 Hoppscotch Teams 与您的团队成员一起创建即时 API 开发环境，或邀请组织外部的协作者为其做出贡献。 管理权限并根据需要分配合适的角色。

跳房子 CLI 💻

设置 Hoppscotch CLI 并通过导出集合和环境来配置您的 API。 按照一组命令获取 API 的测试摘要。

自托管支持🚀

要完全控制您的 API 开发工作流程，请尝试 Hoppscotch 的自托管版本。 在本地计算机上配置和设置 Hoppscotch ，并访问管理仪表板，您可以在其中管理工作区的团队和用户。

这就是一个包装。 探索Hoppscotch 🛸 并随时关注发布内容，以了解我们平台上的最新动态和新内容。

加入我们充满活力的Discord社区。 请填写表格，帮助我们了解 Hoppscotch 如何为您提供帮助，并分享您对我们工作的任何建议。

蒂莫西·斯潘. 一旦被暂停，hoppscotch 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，hoppscotch 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，hoppscotch 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果跳房子没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

使 hoppscotch 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

原文:

Over the past week, we've observed a growing trend of people seeking alternatives to Postman, as they yearn for a more suitable option. ​


​

At Hoppscotch 🛸, we are building an API development ecosystem with a core focus on providing the best developer experience. In this blog, we will explore the features of Hoppscotch and explain how it can enhance your API-building and testing experience.

Open source 👥

​Hoppscotch's open-source nature has helped to keep the contributors, users, and developers in sync. The transparency and trust built over time have led to swiftly addressing the issues, conducting open discussions on feature requests, and delivering a stable and reliable platform.

Collections 📂

As the name suggests, a collection is an organized group of API requests. With Hoppscotch, you can create any number of collections and organize the saved requests inside folders for better navigation.

Multi-Protocol Support ➕

Execute REST API requests, GraphQL queries and work with Real-time protocols like Web Socket, Socket.IO, SSE, and MQTT.

Environments ⚙️

An environment is a reusable set of key-value pairs that can be utilized in creating requests and writing scripts. It also helps to manage the shared data among the team members in a workspace. While accessing the environment variables in Request URL Bar, Hoppscotch offers an edit property to make changes to the variables then and there if needed.

Testing and Scripting Support 🛠

Create test cases and perform test runs on responses you receive after sending requests. Hoppscotch provides JavaScript APIs that can be used in writing pre-request scripts and building tests. Use snippets present at side bar for different fields to know more about them.

History 🕜

Keep track of your API requests and responses with Hoppscotch. As soon as you hit send to initiate a request, the call is recorded by hoppscotch and saved under History. To check out the past requests click on History icon and access when needed.

Team Collaboration 🤝

Use Hoppscotch Teams to create an instant API development environment with your team members or invite collaborators outside your organization to contribute to it. Manage the permissions and assign suitable roles as per need.

Hoppscotch CLI 💻

Set up Hoppscotch CLI and configure your APIs by exporting the collections and environments. Follow a set of commands to get test summary of the API.

Self-Host Support 🚀

To attain complete control of your API development workflow try Hoppscotch's self-host version. Configure and set up Hoppscotch on your local machine and get access to the admin dashboard where you can manage teams and users at your workspace.

And that's a wrap. Explore Hoppscotch 🛸 and stay tuned with releases to keep track of what's cooking and new at our platform.

Join our vibrant community in Discord. Help us understand how Hoppscotch has assisted you and share any suggestions you may have for us to work upon by filling the form.

Timothy Spann. 🇺🇦 - Jun 5

Items.Title: 如果人工智能具有自我意识会怎样🚀🔥 | What If Artificial Intelligence Becomes Self-Aware 🚀🔥
Items.Description:
人工智能（AI）正在迅速发展，其进步激发了研究人员、科学界和公众的好奇心和兴趣。 虽然人工智能目前的能力令人兴奋，但自我意识人工智能的概念引发了有关这种发展的意识和影响的有趣问题。 在本文中，我们探讨了自我意识智能的可能性及其对人类的影响。

人工智能的演变

人工智能可分为狭义智能和通用智能。 狭义人工智能是指设计用于执行具有卓越能力的特定任务（例如文字处理或图像识别）的机器。
另一方面，AGI 的目标是通过使人们能够以多种方式思考和解决问题，从而让人们热爱认知智能。 随着人工智能研究的进展，我们正在接近通用智能的可能性，并提出了为强大机器创造角色的伦理问题。

了解自我意识

人类的自我意识和自我意识已经争论了几个世纪。 他有能力思考思想并认识自己、思想和感受。 在人工智能的背景下，自我意识可以被定义为人工智能对自身处境的认识以及对其环境和行为的理解。
虽然人工智能达到类人意识的可能性还很遥远，但这个想法开启了令人着迷的可能性。

自我意识的挑战和后果

自我意识的概念引发了许多伦理问题。 如果人工智能实现了自我意识，它就能实现正义吗？ 我们如何定义道德责任？ 此外，拥有自我意识的人工智能可以改变人类与人工智能的关系。
随着人工智能系统变得更加自主和有意识，思考人机交互和情感交流的潜力变得很重要。 此外，对人工智能存在的感知会影响决策过程，导致不可预测的后果。

超越图灵测试

图灵测试由艾伦·图灵于 1950 年提出，作为衡量机器再现人类智能能力的基准。 然而，图灵测试并不直接测量自我意识。 随着我们深入研究自我意识人工智能，需要开发针对意识和自我意识的新措施。
保护自我识别人工智能

随着自我意识人工智能的出现，人们对人工智能安全的担忧日益加剧。 为了防止负面后果，确保良好的安全性非常重要。 重要的是要确保人工智能系统是根据人类价值观设计的，并且行为符合道德规范。 此外，人工智能研究人员、社会工作者和政策制定者之间的合作可以帮助创建人工智能责任发展的框架。

期望和猜测

如果人工智能能够提供自我意识，那将非常有用。
自我意识人工智能可以通过更好地了解人们的需求来实现更好的理解和人际关系。 它可以通过提供独特的视角和见解来彻底改变医学、教育和科学等领域。 然而，对人工智能自我意识未来的预测必须谨慎，因为需要谨慎管理道德问题和社会影响。

结论

人工智能中自我意识的可能性是一个扩展人工智能研究领域的伟大概念。 虽然我们目前距离实现真正的自我意识人工智能还很远，但必须仔细考虑人工智能发展之路的影响。
负责任的人工智能发展、道德和合作伙伴关系是进步的关键，并确保人工智能发展植根于创新和责任。 当我们踏上这一旅程时，我们必须以兴奋和谨慎的平衡来拥抱人工智能的未来。 评论你的想法！

暂时有一些多汁的模因！



原文:

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is evolving rapidly, and its progress has sparked the curiosity and interest of researchers, science, and the public. While the current capabilities of AI are exciting, the concept of self-aware AI raises interesting questions about the consciousness and implications of this development. In this article, we explore the possibility of self-aware intelligence and its impact on humans.

The Evolution of Artificial Intelligence

Artificial Intelligence can be divided into narrow intelligence and general intelligence. Narrow AI refers to machines designed to perform specific tasks with superior abilities such as word processing or image recognition.
AGI, on the other hand, aims to make people love cognitive intelligence by enabling them to think and problem solve in many ways. As AI research progresses, we are approaching the possibility of general intelligence and raising ethical concerns for creating a role for powerful machines.

Understanding Self-Awareness

Human self-awareness and self-awareness have been debated for centuries. He has the ability to think about thoughts and recognize himself, thoughts and feelings. In the context of artificial intelligence, self-awareness can be defined as the artificial intelligence's recognizing its own situation and understanding its environment and behavior.
While artificial intelligence reaching human-like consciousness is a remote possibility, the idea opens up fascinating possibilities.

The challenges and consequences of self-awareness

The concept of self-awareness raises many ethical issues. If AI achieves self-awareness, will it achieve justice? How do we define moral responsibility? Additionally, having self-aware AI could change the human relationship with AI.
As AI systems become more autonomous and conscious, it becomes important to think about the potential for human-machine interaction and emotional communication. In addition, the perception of the existence of artificial intelligence can affect the decision-making process, leading to unpredictable consequences.

Beyond The Turing Test

Turing Test was proposed by Alan Turing in 1950 as a benchmark for measuring a machine's ability to reproduce human intelligence. However, the Turing test does not directly measure self-awareness. As we delve deeper into self-aware AI, new measures specific to consciousness and self-awareness will need to be developed.
Protecting Self-Recognizing Artificial Intelligence

With the possibility of self-aware AI, concerns about AI security are intensifying. It is important to ensure good security in order to prevent negative consequences. It is important to ensure that artificial intelligence systems are designed according to human values ​​and act ethically. Additionally, collaboration between AI researchers, social workers, and policy makers can help create a framework for the development of AI responsibility.

Expectations and Speculations

It will be very useful if artificial intelligence can provide self-awareness.
Self-aware AI enables greater understanding and personal relationships by better understanding people's needs. It can revolutionise fields such as medicine, education, and science by providing unique perspectives and insights. However, predictions about the future of AI self-awareness must be made with caution, as ethical concerns and social impact need to be carefully managed.

Conclusion

The possibility of self-awareness in AI is a great concept that expands the field of AI research. While we are currently far from achieving truly self-aware AI, the path to AI development must be carefully considered in terms of its implications.
Responsible AI development, ethical and collaborative partnerships are key to progress and ensure that AI development is rooted in innovation and responsibility. As we embark on this journey, we must embrace the future of artificial intelligence with a balance of excitement and caution. Comment your thoughts!

Items.Title: 使用 Swagger 和 OpenAPI 设计 API | Designing APIs with Swagger and OpenAPI
Items.Description:
免责声明：这篇文章包含附属链接； 如果您通过本文中提供的不同链接购买这本书，我可能会收到补偿。

这篇评论是关于使用 Swagger 和 OpenAPI 设计 API，作者是 Manning 的 Joshua S. Ponelat 和 Lukas L. Rosenstock。

我将通过阅读书籍、观看相关 YouTube 视频和阅读相关 IETF RFC 来继续我的 API 之旅。

今天是书评。

事实

  • 章节
    • 第 1 部分：描述 API
      1. 准备发出 API 请求
      2. 我们第一次体验 OpenAPI 定义
      3. 使用 Swagger Editor 编写 OpenAPI 定义
      4. 描述 API 响应
      5. 创建资源
      6. 添加认证和授权
      7. 准备和托管 API 文档
    • 第 2 部分：设计第一
      1. 设计网络应用程序
      2. 使用 OpenAPI 创建 API 设计
      3. 首先围绕 API 设计构建变更工作流程
      4. 实现前端代码并对更改做出反应
      5. 使用 Node.js 和 Swagger Codegen 构建后端
      6. 集成并发布Web应用程序
    • 第 3 部分：扩展 API
      1. 设计下一个 API 迭代
      2. 在 OpenAPI 中设计具有组合的模式
      3. 使用过滤器和分页扩展集合端点
      4. 支持不幸的路径：使用 Problem+json 进行错误处理
      5. 使用高级 JSON 架构改进输入验证
      6. 对 API 进行版本控制并处理重大更改
      7. 本书通过演示项目Farmstall API设计了一个完整的 API。

        优点和缺点

        至少可以说，这篇评论很简洁。

        这本书的主要好处也是它的主要问题：它专注于初学者。 一切都非常详细。 例如，作者在第 11 章专门解释了 Git 的基础知识。

        我不是API专家，但我没有学到任何东西。 因此，我没什么可说的。

        结论

        如果您是一个真正的新手，即您对 HTTP、请求和响应、OpenAPI 和 Postman 一无所知，那么这本书适合您。 它从头开始详细解释了一切。

        如果您对以上任何一项都只是一知半解，恐怕这将是您金钱和时间的损失。

原文:

Disclaimer: this post includes affiliate links; I may receive compensation if you purchase the book from the different links provided in this post.

This review is about Designing APIs with Swagger and OpenAPI by Joshua S. Ponelat and Lukas L. Rosenstock from Manning.

I'm continuing my API journey by reading books, viewing relevant YouTube videos, and reading relevant IETF RFCs.

Today is a book review.

Facts

  • 21 chapters
  • $38.39 (eBook)

Chapters

  • Part 1: Describing APIs
    1. Introducing APIs and OpenAPI
    2. Getting set up to make API requests
    3. Our first taste of OpenAPI definitions
    4. Using Swagger Editor to write OpenAPI definitions
    5. Describing API responses
    6. Creating resources
    7. Adding authentication and authorization
    8. Preparing and hosting API documentation
  • Part 2: Design first
    1. Designing a web application
    2. Creating an API design using OpenAPI
    3. Building a change workflow around API design–first
    4. Implementing frontend code and reacting to changes
    5. Building a backend with Node.js and Swagger Codegen
    6. Integrating and releasing the web application
  • Part 3: Extending APIs
    1. Designing the next API iteration
    2. Designing schemas with composition in OpenAPI
    3. Scaling collection endpoints with filters and pagination
    4. Supporting the unhappy path: Error handling with problem+json
    5. Improving input validation with advanced JSON Schema
    6. Versioning an API and handling breaking changes
    7. The API prerelease checklist

The book goes through designing a complete API via a demo project, the Farmstall API.

Pros and cons

The review is concise, to say the least.

The book's main benefit is also its main issue: it focuses on beginners. Everything is very detailed. For example, the authors dedicated chapter 11 to explaining Git's basics.

I'm not an API expert, but I didn't learn anything. Hence, I don't have a lot to say.

Conclusion

If you're a true newbie, i.e., you know nothing about HTTP, requests and responses, OpenAPI, and Postman, this book is for you. It goes into great detail in explaining everything from scratch.

If you have more than a passing familiarity with any of the above, I'm afraid it will be a loss of your money and time.

Originally published at A Java Geek on July 16th, 2023

Items.Title: PHP 8 中的设计模式：享元 | Design Patterns in PHP 8: Flyweight
Items.Description:
各位开发者大家好！

今天，我们将深入研究另一种令人着迷的设计模式，它可以帮助我们编写更高效、可扩展的 PHP 代码。 如果您发现自己正在处理大量相似的对象，并且想知道如何更有效地管理它们，那么您将会很高兴。 我们将探索享元模式，这是一种旨在更有效地使用内存的结构设计模式。

享元模式就像一位伪装大师。 它允许我们通过与相似对象共享尽可能多的数据来最大限度地减少内存使用。 这就像一个特工改变了一些外表细节以采用新的身份，但他们的核心特征保持不变。 在我们的 PHP 代码上下文中，享元模式允许我们毫不费力地处理数千甚至数百万个对象。

在本文中，我们将带您走进电子商务的世界。 我们将了解如何使用享元模式来更有效地管理产品变体，从而节省内存并提高性能。 所以，系好安全带，让我们开始吧！

例如，让我们考虑一个销售 T 恤的电子商务平台。 每件 T 恤都有不同的尺寸和颜色，但所有相同设计的 T 恤都有一些共同的属性，例如产品描述、图像和价格。 共享数据可以移出到一个单独的享元对象中，该对象可以在所有 T 恤对象之间共享。

 class TShirtFlyweight { private string $description; private string $image; private float $price; public function __construct(string $description, string $image, float $price) { $this->description = $description; $this->image = $image; $this->price = $price; } // Getters for description, image, and price... }

在此代码中， TShirtFlyweight是包含共享状态（在本例中为产品描述、图像和价格）的 Flyweight 类。

 class TShirt { private TShirtFlyweight $flyweight; private string $color; private string $size; public function __construct(TShirtFlyweight $flyweight, string $color, string $size) { $this->flyweight = $flyweight; $this->color = $color; $this->size = $size; } // Getters for color, size, and flyweight... }

所有 T 恤（ $tshirt1 、 $tshirt2 、 $tshirt3 ）都具有相同的描述、图像和价格，但颜色和尺寸不同。

在我们的电子商务示例中，我们了解了如何使用享元模式更有效地管理产品变体，从而节省内存并提高性能。 这就像有一个超级高效的仓库管理员，他确切地知道所有东西在哪里，并且可以立即检索它。

我们刚刚深入研究了享元模式的世界，探索它如何帮助我们在 PHP 中更有效地管理大量相似的对象。 这就像我们的武器库中拥有一件秘密武器，可以让我们毫不费力地处理数千甚至数百万个物体。

请记住，像享元这样的设计模式并不是一刀切的解决方案。 它们是我们开发人员工具箱中的工具，了解何时以及如何使用它们可以区分正常工作的代码和高效、优雅地工作的代码。

我希望您发现对享元模式的探索有帮助和启发。 一如既往，我鼓励您卷起袖子，亲自编写一些代码，并尝试在自己的项目中实现享元模式。

我目前正在写一本广泛涵盖这些主题的书。 它充满了实际示例、清晰的解释以及可以应用这些模式的现实场景。

本书旨在帮助初学者和经验丰富的开发人员深入了解设计模式以及如何在PHP和TypeScript中实现它们。 无论您是想温习知识还是学习新知识，这本书都能满足您的需求。

您可以在 dev.to 上订阅我的博客，然后一旦这本书准备好您就会收到通知。 我迫不及待地想让您阅读它并将您的编码技能提升到一个新的水平！

原文:

Hello, fellow developers!

Today, we're going to dive into another fascinating design pattern that can help us write more efficient and scalable PHP code. If you've ever found yourself dealing with a large number of similar objects and wondered how to manage them more efficiently, then you're in for a treat. We're going to explore the Flyweight pattern, a structural design pattern that's all about using memory more efficiently.

The Flyweight pattern is like a master of disguise. It allows us to minimize memory usage by sharing as much data as possible with similar objects. It's like a secret agent who changes a few details about their appearance to take on a new identity, while their core characteristics remain the same. In the context of our PHP code, the Flyweight pattern allows us to handle thousands, or even millions, of objects without breaking a sweat.

In this article, we'll be taking a trip to the world of e-commerce. We'll see how the Flyweight pattern can be used to manage product variations more efficiently, saving memory and improving performance. So, buckle up and let's get started!

For the example, let's consider an e-commerce platform that sells T-shirts. Each T-shirt comes in different sizes and colors, but all T-shirts of the same design share some common attributes like product description, image, and price. The shared data can be moved out into a separate flyweight object, which can be shared among all T-shirt objects.

class TShirtFlyweight
{ private string $description; private string $image; private float $price; public function __construct(string $description, string $image, float $price) { $this->description = $description; $this->image = $image; $this->price = $price; } // Getters for description, image, and price...
}

In this code, TShirtFlyweight is the Flyweight class that contains the shared state (in this case, the product description, image, and price).

class TShirt
{ private TShirtFlyweight $flyweight; private string $color; private string $size; public function __construct(TShirtFlyweight $flyweight, string $color, string $size) { $this->flyweight = $flyweight; $this->color = $color; $this->size = $size; } // Getters for color, size, and flyweight...
}

TShirt is the class that contains the unique state (in this case, the color and size of the T-shirt).

class TShirtFactory
{ private array $flyweights = []; public function getFlyweight(string $description, string $image, float $price): TShirtFlyweight { $key = md5($description . $image . $price); if (!isset($this->flyweights[$key])) { $this->flyweights[$key] = new TShirtFlyweight($description, $image, $price); } return $this->flyweights[$key]; }
}

TShirtFactory is used to create and manage flyweight objects.

$factory = new TShirtFactory();
$flyweight = $factory->getFlyweight('Cool T-Shirt', 'image.png', 19.99); $tshirt1 = new TShirt($flyweight, 'red', 'M');
$tshirt2 = new TShirt($flyweight, 'blue', 'L');
$tshirt3 = new TShirt($flyweight, 'green', 'S');

All T-shirts ($tshirt1, $tshirt2, $tshirt3) share the same description, image, and price, but have different colors and sizes.

In our e-commerce example, we saw how the Flyweight pattern can be used to manage product variations more efficiently, saving memory and improving performance. It's like having a super-efficient warehouse manager who knows exactly where everything is and can retrieve it in an instant.

We've just taken a deep dive into the world of the Flyweight pattern, exploring how it can help us manage a large number of similar objects more efficiently in PHP. It's like having a secret weapon in our arsenal that allows us to handle thousands, or even millions, of objects without breaking a sweat.

Remember, design patterns like the Flyweight are not one-size-fits-all solutions. They are tools in our developer toolbox, and knowing when and how to use them can make the difference between code that just works and code that works efficiently and elegantly.

I hope you found this exploration of the Flyweight pattern helpful and enlightening. As always, I encourage you to roll up your sleeves, get your hands dirty with some code, and try implementing the Flyweight pattern in your own projects.

P.S. If you found this article helpful and want to dive deeper into design patterns in PHP and TypeScript, I have some exciting news for you! I'm currently writing a book that extensively covers these topics. It's packed with practical examples, clear explanations, and real-world scenarios where these patterns can be applied.

The book is designed to help both beginners and experienced developers gain a solid understanding of design patterns and how to implement them in PHP and TypeScript. Whether you're looking to brush up on your knowledge or learn something new, this book has got you covered.

You can subscribe to my blog on dev.to and then you will receive a notification as soon as the book is ready. I can't wait for you to read it and take your coding skills to the next level!

Items.Title: 作为入门级数据分析师/科学家，您可以尝试的 10 个组合项目 | 10 Portfolio Projects you can try as an entry-level Data Analyst/Scientist
Items.Description:
我讨厌新手这个词。 如果你赶时间，请跳到第三段。 在直奔主题之前，我总是先做“赶上”的事情。

在我的旅程中，以下是我参与的 10 个项目，以构建我的投资组合/职业生涯。

使用的工具：Python、HTML、CSS、Flask、基本 ML 知识
难度：简单

这是我做过的第一个项目，尽管我现在非常讨厌它，但我为它感到自豪。 我建立了一个决策树模型，可以推荐特定天气和土壤条件下的最佳作物。 我使用 Flask 在本地部署了它，目前我的 github 上有一个糟糕的项目版本，所以我不想链接它。 当我推送更好的版本时，我会将其链接到这里。

使用的工具：Python、NLTK 和余弦相似度知识、Heroku、Streamlit
难度：中等

现在，这是我的第二个项目，但它与第一​​个项目完全不同。 它使用 NLP 和余弦相似度。 我刚刚在 Coursera 上完成 Andrew Ng 的机器学习课程，并在 YouTube 上观看了 TMDB 电影推荐教程，因此我在 Netflix 数据集上构建了一个。 我还致力于 Streamlit 以允许用户访问，甚至使用 Heroku 进行部署。 对我来说，这是我做过的最困难的项目。 我什至哭了。 目前，我已经学会了更好的做事方法，但我确实从中学到了很多东西。这是github的链接。 它需要一些整理，但并没有那么糟糕。

使用的工具：Python（Pandas 和 Matplolib）
难度：简单

这是我发布的第一个 EDA 项目。 我也在此链接上写过相关内容。 这个项目很简单，它让我意识到你也可以从小项目中学习。 我复习了 Pandas 和 Matplolib 的知识。 我还学会了如何提出正确的问题，以及如何有针对性地进行分析以揭示某些内容。 很多人也通过这个项目认识了我。 这是该项目的GitHub链接。

使用工具：Python(pandas、matplotlib、关联规则)
难度：中等

我还没有发布关于这个项目的文章，但这是我认为数据分析师应该尝试的项目之一。 您将了解关联规则、公司中的产品如何销售以及哪些产品最适合相互销售。 高销量产品如何帮助销售低销量产品等等。 我喜欢学习和做这件事，并且可能很快就会将其推送到我的 GitHub 上，但在此之前你应该研究并尝试一下。 这很容易。

使用的工具：Python
难度：中等

现在，这不是一个与数据相关的项目。 去年我参加了一个面向 Python 的学院课程，并且我很幸运地用 Python 实现了这个算法。 这个算法太有趣了。 最终目标是每个人都与（男人）/（女人）结婚，并且他们都对自己的婚姻感到满意。 他们都可以选择最方便的选择。 我认为我解释得不够好。 我可能会专门写一篇文章来讨论它，但在此之前，你可以在谷歌上阅读/研究它。

使用的工具：Tableau、Python（用于分析）
难度：简单

贝克德尔测试确定电影中至少存在一个女人与另一个女人交谈的场景，但与男人无关。 我一定会写一篇关于这个项目的文章。 这是我第一次阅读时就着迷的作品之一。 当我听说这个测试时，我想用它做点什么来告诉人们。 我将其与女权主义的演变联系起来，并研究女权主义的影响是否改善了社会对女性的看法。 因此，我将这些年份分为不同的世纪，并观察这些年来通过测试的电影数量。 我什至为它做了一个可视化的画面，但我还没有完善它。 我也没有发布过相关内容。

工具：Python、NLTK、Power BI
难度：简单

当《黑豹 2》问世时，我做了一个情感分析项目，最近我又用两个不同的库做了另一个项目。 这很容易做到，我认为这是每个数据分析师都应该尝试的事情。 我什至使用 Power BI 对其进行可视化，并且我敢于使用黑色背景。 是的。 我就是这么做的。 这是帖子的链接： 黑豹。

使用的工具：PostgreSQL、Excel、Power BI
难度：中等

这又是让我脱颖而出的项目之一。 我收到了很多关于这个项目的评论和反馈。 我使用 SQL、Excel 进行清理，使用 Power BI 进行可视化。 我已经写过相关文章并在此链接上发布了它。 数据是从此链接获取的，我根据数据专业人员的职业、流动性、就业类型等探索了他们的薪资。 我使用过窗口函数和子查询，老实说，我能够正确地练习我所学到的东西。

使用工具：Python
难度： 困难

这是我参与过的最困难的项目之一。我构建了使用电子邮件结构、样式特征等对网络钓鱼电子邮件和非网络钓鱼电子邮件进行分类的模型。 这花了相当长的时间。 我从事特征提取、数据清理、降维、交叉验证和模型构建方面的工作。 也探索了不同的评价方法。 我还没有将其推送到我的 GitHub 上，但我很快就会这么做。 但我认为我不能就此发表帖子。

还有更多项目需要讨论，但排名第 10 的项目将是为开源做出贡献。 我通过开源学习了单元测试、git 等等。 这是我不常做的事情，因为我总是有一些小工作让我很忙，但一旦我有了一份全职工作，我肯定会成为一名定期贡献者。 有很多东西需要学习，开源是学习它们的最快方法之一。

与我联系：
领英： www.linkedin.com/in/durgesh4993/
推特： twitter.com/durgesh4993



原文:

I hate the word newbie. If you are in a hurry, skip to the third paragraph. I always do this “catching up” thing before going straight to the point.

In the course of my journey, here are 10 projects I had engaged in to build my portfolio/career.

1.Crop Recommendation System

Tools used: Python, HTML, CSS, Flask, Basic ML knowledge
Difficulty: Easy

This was the first project I ever did and even though I hate it so much now, I’m so proud of it. I built a decision tree model that recommends the best crop under certain weather and soil condition. I deployed it locally using Flask and I have a terrible version of the project on my github currently so I do not want to link it. When I push a better version, I will link it here.

2.Movie recommender system

Tools used: Python, Knowledge of NLTK and Cosine Similarity, Heroku, Streamlit
Difficulty: Medium

Now, this was my second project but it was nothing like the first project. It uses NLP and cosine similarity. I had just finished Andrew Ng’s Machine learning course on Coursera and watched a TMDB movie recommender tutorial on YouTube so I built one on the Netflix dataset. I also worked on streamlit to allow user access and even deployed using Heroku. For me, this is the hardest project I have ever done. I even cried. Currently, I have learned better ways to do things but I did learn a lot from it.This is a link to the github. It needs some tidying but it’s not that terrible.

3. Forbes 2022 EDA using Python

Tools used: Python (Pandas and Matplolib)
Difficulty: Easy

This was the first EDA project that I published. I had written about it too on this link. The project was easy, it made me realize you learn from small projects too. I revised my knowledge of Pandas and Matplolib. I also learned how to ask the right questions, and how analysis is targeted toward uncovering something. A whole lot of people got to know me through this project too. This is a GitHub link to the project.

4. Market Basket Analysis

Tools used: Python(pandas, matplotlib, association rules)
Difficulty: Medium

I haven't posted about this project yet but it’s one of the projects I think a data analyst should try. You get to understand association rules, how products in a company sell, and which products are best sold with each other. How a high-sales product can aid in selling a low-sales one and so on. I enjoyed learning and doing this one and might be pushing it on my GitHub soon but before then you should research and try it. It is easy.

5. Implementing Gayle-Shapley’s Stable Matching Algorithm

Tools Used: Python
Difficulty: Medium

Now, this isn’t a data-related project. I went for an academy program last year that is python oriented and I was opportune enough to implement this algorithm in python. This algorithm is so interesting. The Gayle-Sharply matching algorithm is aimed at ensuring stable matching. The end goal is meant to be that everyone gets married to a (man)/(woman) and they are all happy with their matches. They all get to be with their most available preference. I don’t think I am explaining it well enough. I might dedicate a whole post to it but before then, you can read/research about it on google.

6. The Bechdel test

Tools used: Tableau, Python (For analysis)
Difficulty: Easy

The Bechdel Test ascertains there exists at least a scene in a movie where a woman speaks to another woman and it isn’t about a man. I will definitely write a post about this project. It’s one of the ones that hooked me on the first read. The moment I heard of this test, I wanted to do something with it to tell people about it. I linked it with the evolution of feminism and researched if the impact of feminism has improved how society viewed women. As such, I grouped the years into different centuries and observed the number of movies that passed the test over the years. I even made a tableau visualization for it but I haven’t perfected it yet. I haven’t posted about it either.

7. Sentiment Analysis Project

Tools: Python, NLTK, Power BI
Difficulty: Easy

I had done a sentiment analysis project when black panther 2 came out and I did another recently with two different libraries. It’s quite easy to do and I think it’s something every data analyst should try. I even visualized it using Power BI and I dared to use a black background. Yes. I did that. Here is a link to the post: Black Panther.

8. Data science job salaries

Tools Used: PostgreSQL, Excel, Power BI
Difficulty: Medium

Again, one of the projects that made me out there. I got so many reviews and feedback on this project. I used SQL, Excel for cleaning, and Power BI for visualization. I had written about it and published it too on this link. The data was gotten from this link and I explored the salaries of data professionals by their professions, mobility, employment type, and many more. SQL was used for the data analysis. I had used window functions and subqueries and honestly, I was able to properly practice what I had learned.

9. Classification of a phishing mail

Tools used: Python
Difficulty: Hard

This is one of the toughest projects I have engaged in. I built models that classify phishing emails and non-phishing emails using email structure, stylometric features, and so on. It took quite a time. I worked on feature extraction, data cleaning, dimensionality reduction, cross-validation, and model building. explored different evaluation methods too. I haven't pushed this on my GitHub either but I will soon. I don’t think I can make a post about it though.

10. Open Source Contribution

There are still some more projects to talk about but the number 10 project will be to contribute to open source. I learned unit testing, git and so much more through open source. It is something I don't do often because I always have little jobs that keep me so occupied but once I have a full-time job, I will definitely become a regular contributor. There is so much to learn and open source is one of the fastest ways to learn them.

Time for #DEVDiscuss — right here on DEV 😎

Inspired by @abhixsh's Top 7 post, tonight’s topic is... package management!

If you're not familiar, a package manager is a system that will manage your project dependencies. A dependency is a third-party bit of software, usually written by someone else, that solves a problem for you. Node Package Manager (abbreviated to NPM) is a robust package manager designed for JavaScript projects, primarily used in conjunction with Node.js.

Items.Title: 🧑‍🎓 如何选择适合你的学习方式 | 🧑‍🎓 How to Choose the Right Learning Style for You
Items.Description:
还记得在学校时老师会解释一些事情然后提出问题的日子吗？

班上总会有一个孩子刚刚得到它。

就好像他们有过目不忘的记忆力📸。

他们只需要见一个人一次，就会一拍即合！

也许你很幸运能成为那个孩子。 如果是这样，那么您可能不一定需要这篇文章（但我保证它仍然值得一读）。

如果你不是那个孩子，那么你可能会像我一样努力寻找学习方法来让信息记住。

因为在教育方面有一点是肯定的：并不是每个人都学得一样。

这就是个人学习风格的重要性发挥作用的时候。

什么是个人学习风格？

个人学习风格本质上是我们吸收、处理和保留信息的偏好。

如果我们能够自己认识和理解这些不同的学习方式，我们就可以显着提高学习和应用新知识的效率，特别是在像编程这样多样化和广泛的领域。

有 4 种主要的学习方式，您能找出哪一种最适合您吗？

视觉学习风格👀

如果您是视觉学习者，那么当您可以看到或想象信息时，您可以最好地吸收信息。

您更喜欢图形、图表、流程图和其他视觉辅助工具来帮助您在脑海中描绘概念。

在学习编码时，视觉学习者可能会发现说明程序逻辑或数据流的流程图或图表特别有用。

您还往往会受益于提供可视化反馈、突出显示语法错误并提供可视化调试工具的集成开发环境 ( IDE )。

听觉学习风格👂

如果你是听觉学习者，当你听到信息时，你就会进步。

您可以从讨论、口头讲座和录音中受益。

在编程的背景下，听觉学习者可能更喜欢视频教程，其中讲师口头解释概念，或者播客介绍编码概念和实践。

这种以听觉为中心的资源可以使复杂的编程概念对您来说更容易理解，因为您通常具有理解和记住口头信息的技巧。

阅读/写作学习方式✍️

如果您有阅读/写作学习偏好，您会倾向于文本信息。

通过阅读和做详细笔记，您可以最好地吸收和保留知识。

编程书籍、在线文章、API 文档和书面教程可以成为您的金矿。

在学习编码时，您可能会发现编写伪代码或仔细记录代码很有帮助，有助于您理解和回忆编程概念。

动觉学习风格🧑‍💻

如果您是动觉学习者，那么通过身体体验和实践活动可以学得最好。

当您在学习过程中能够操纵、搭建、触摸或移动时，您就会表现出色。

对于学习编程，这可能意味着深入进行编码练习、构建项目或使用交互式学习平台。

您可能会发现，当您可以“玩”代码、实时实验并从错误中学习时，您的理解会加深。

认识您的学习风格是了解哪种学习风格最适合您的第一步。

然而，这些风格并不相互排斥。

我们中的许多人都是混合学习者，这意味着我们可以将多种风格的各个方面纳入我们独特的学习方法中。

那也完全没问题！

在我们进一步创建您独特的学习方法之前，让我们先看看如何选择正确的学习资源。

如何选择合适的学习资源

学习资源和学习方式一样多种多样。

了解哪些工具可以补充您的学习风格可以让您的编程学习之旅更加顺利和愉快。

以下是一些关于您可以根据您的学习风格使用的资源类型的提示：

视觉学习者资源📼

视觉学习者通常会利用将文本代码转化为交互式、引人入胜的视觉效果的资源来茁壮成长。

在线视频教程（例如YouTube或Coursera和Udemy等平台上的视频教程）是一个很好的资源。

这些平台的讲师经常在编写代码时解释概念，让学习者能够直观地理解。

提供编码挑战的网站通常有视觉表示来帮助您更好地理解问题。

听觉学习者资源 🎧

对于听觉学习者来说，利用声音的资源可以帮助更深入地理解编程概念。

就像视觉学习者一样，视频讲座或网络研讨会可以为听觉学习者提供聆听专家口头解释编程原理的好处。

阅读/写作学习者的资源📚

阅读/写作学习者可以在基于文本的资源中找到广泛的信息。

编程书籍是获取深入知识的重要资源。 而Dev.to 、 Hashnode或Stack Overflow等网站提供了各种关于编码问题的文章和答案。

阅读官方文档、写笔记或维护编码日志也可以增强对信息的理解和保留。

动觉学习者资源💻

当动觉学习者亲自接触代码时，他们会蓬勃发展。

基于项目的学习，例如构建个人网站或为开源项目做出贡献，允许学习者在现实场景中应用概念。

使用Replit或Glitch等工具，您可以在编码项目上进行编写、共享和协作，可以进一步支持边做边学。

将您的学习风格与正确的资源相匹配将改变您的学习旅程，并帮助您避免诸如教程地狱之类的事情。

如前所述，如果您喜欢混合使用这些样式，那完全没问题，所以让我们看看将它们混合在一起会是什么样子：

混合学习方式

大多数人并不严格遵循任何单一风格。

相反，我们经常使用多种风格的混合，根据当时的主题、背景或个人喜好调整我们的方法。

例如，虽然您可能喜欢阅读编程书籍（阅读/写作风格），但您也可以通过观看教程来查看正在运行的代码（视觉风格）或在通勤期间收听同一主题的播客（听觉风格）来发现价值。

整合不同的学习方法并混合资源可以帮助建立对编程的强大和全面的理解。

阅读一个概念可以提供理论知识（阅读/写作），而看到它的实际应用可以加深理解（视觉）。

与同事讨论（听觉）或将其应用到项目中（动觉）可以巩固知识，将其转化为实用技能。

混合学习方式的示例

假设您想了解 Web 开发。

您首先阅读有关 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript（阅读/写作）的书籍或在线文章。 然后，您可以观看 YouTube 教程或在线课程，直观地演示这些语言如何协同工作来构建网站（视觉）。

最后，您通过创建自己的网站（动觉）来测试您的知识。

这些资源的结合带来更全面的学习体验，让您从不同角度了解 Web 开发。

它还表明，虽然确定你的主要学习风格是有益的，但灵活性和适应不同学习方法的能力对于你掌握编程的道路同样重要。

就我个人而言，我喜欢将阅读与动觉结合起来。

我总是首先寻求文档，并且更喜欢书面的分步指南而不是观看视频。

我发现视频放慢了我的速度，我只想进入建筑部分！

因此，如果我可以通读教程，我可以直接阅读我认为最有用的部分，甚至可以突出显示并保存它们以供以后使用。

当我观看视频时，我更喜欢纪录片或讲座风格的学习，而不是“代码跟随” - 但嘿，这就是我！

最棒的是，可用的资源非常多，当你找到自己喜欢的风格时，总有可能有与之匹配的资源。

结论

每个人的编程之路都与这个人一样独特。

个性化您的学习之旅不仅仅是选择编程语言或决定下一步构建哪个项目。

它包括了解如何最有效地学习并选择符合该风格的资源。

了解您的学习风格可以极大地提高您的学习过程的效率。

您可以选择与您自然学习方式相辅相成的材料，而不是苦苦寻找与您的风格不符的资源。

通过这样做，您就为成功做好了准备，确保您不仅学习，而且真正理解并享受学习如何编码的过程。

当您继续您的编程之旅时，请尝试探索不同的资源，尝试各种学习策略，并发现最适合您的方法。

不要害怕走出你的舒适区——这往往是最显着的成长发生的地方。

花点时间反思您的学习经历，随着您对自己和编程这片神奇土地的更多了解，调整您的策略。

找到你独特的风格！

来自不断成长的开发者同伴，

查洛克代码

我向不断成长的开发人员社区发布每周简讯，寻求提高编程技能并继续不断自我完善的旅程。 重点关注提高编程效率的技巧🚀。

将更多类似的文章直接发送到您的收件箱。

让我们一起成长吧🌱

并通过 🐦 @evergrinddev保持联系



原文:

Remember the days back in school when the teacher would explain something and then ask questions?

And there would always be that one kid in the class that just got it.

It’s almost as though they had a photographic memory 📸.

They only had to see someone once, and it would click!

Maybe you were lucky enough to be that kid. If so then you may not necessarily need this article (but it’s still worth reading, I promise).

If you weren’t that kid, then like me, you may have struggled to find ways of learning things that would make the info stick.

Because there’s one thing for sure when it comes to education: not everybody learns the same.

That’s when the importance of personal learning styles comes into play.

What are Personal Learning Styles?

Personal learning styles are essentially preferences for how we absorb, process, and retain information.

If we can recognise and understand these different styles of learning for ourselves, we can significantly enhance how effectively we learn and apply new knowledge, especially in a field as diverse and wide as programming.

There are 4 main styles of learning, can you spot which one might suit you best?

Visual Learning Style 👀

If you’re a visual learner, you absorb information best when you can see or visualise it.

You prefer graphics, diagrams, flow charts, and other visual aids that help you picture concepts in your mind.

When learning to code, visual learners might find flowcharts or diagrams illustrating program logic or data flow particularly useful.

You also tend to benefit from integrated development environments (IDEs) that provide visual feedback, highlighting syntax errors and offering visual debugging tools.

Auditory Learning Style👂

If you’re an auditory learner, you thrive when you hear information.

You benefit from discussions, spoken lectures, and audio recordings.

In the context of programming, auditory learners might prefer video tutorials where an instructor explains the concept verbally or podcasts that go into coding concepts and practices.

Such auditory-focused resources can make complex programming concepts more digestible for you as you often have a knack for understanding and remembering spoken information.

Reading/Writing Learning Style ✍️

If you have a reading/writing learning preference, you gravitate towards textual information.

You absorb and retain knowledge best through reading and taking detailed notes.

Programming books, online articles, API documentation, and written tutorials can be goldmines for you.

When learning to code, you might find it beneficial to write out pseudocode or document your code meticulously, assisting your understanding and recall of programming concepts.

Kinesthetic Learning Style 🧑‍💻

If you’re a kinesthetic learner, you learn best through physical experience and hands-on activities.

You excel when you can manipulate, build, touch, or move during the learning process.

For learning programming, this could mean diving into coding exercises, building projects, or using interactive learning platforms.

You might find that your understanding deepens when you can 'play' with the code, experimenting and learning from your mistakes in real time.

Recognising your learning style is the first step towards understanding which one suits you best.

However, these styles are not mutually exclusive.

Many of us are hybrid learners, meaning that we can take aspects from multiple styles into our unique learning approach.

And that’s totally fine too!

Before we go any further into creating your unique learning approach, let’s look at how to choose the right learning resources.

How to Choose the Right Learning Resources

Learning resources are just as varied as learning styles.

Knowing which tools complement your learning style can make your journey in learning programming smoother and more enjoyable.

Here are some tips for the types of resources you can use based on your learning style:

Resources for Visual Learners 📼

Visual learners often thrive with resources that transform textual code into interactive, engaging visuals.

Online video tutorials, such as those found on YouTube or platforms like Coursera and Udemy, are a great resource for this.

These platforms have instructors that often explain concepts while writing code, allowing learners to follow along visually.

Websites that offer coding challenges often have visual representations to help you understand problems better.

And diagramming tools can be used to create flowcharts or system designs that can further enhance understanding.

Resources for Auditory Learners 🎧

For auditory learners, resources that leverage sound can help with understanding programming concepts more deeply.

Podcasts such as CodeNewbie, Programming Throwdown, or Syntax provide verbal insights into the programming world, and platforms like Audible have audiobooks on coding concepts.

Just like with visual learners, video lectures or webinars can offer auditory learners the benefit of hearing experts explain programming principles verbally.

Resources for Reading/Writing Learners 📚

Reading/Writing learners can find a wide range of information in text-based resources.

Programming books are a great resource to get in-depth knowledge. Whereas websites like Dev.to, Hashnode, or Stack Overflow provide a variety of articles and answers to coding problems.

Reading through official documentation, writing notes, or maintaining a coding journal can also enhance understanding and retention of information.

Resources for Kinesthetic Learners 💻

Kinesthetic learners flourish when they get their hands dirty with code.

Interactive websites like Codecademy or freeCodeCamp offer hands-on coding exercises that provide immediate feedback.

Project-based learning, such as building a personal website or contributing to open-source projects, allows learners to apply concepts in real-world scenarios.

Using tools like Replit or Glitch, where you can write, share, and collaborate on coding projects, can further support learning through doing.

Matching your learning style to the right resources will transform your learning journey, and help you avoid things like tutorial hell.

As mentioned before it’s perfectly okay if you prefer a mixture of these styles, so let’s take a look at how blending them together could look like:

Blending Learning Styles

Most people don't strictly follow any single style.

Instead, we often use a blend of styles, adjusting our approach based on the subject matter, context, or personal preference at that moment in time.

For example, while you might enjoy reading a programming book (Reading/Writing style), you could also find value in watching a tutorial to see the code in action (Visual style) or listening to a podcast on the same topic during your commute (Auditory style).

Integrating different learning methods and blending resources can help build a strong and all-round understanding of programming.

Reading about a concept provides theoretical knowledge (Reading/Writing) while seeing it in action deepens understanding (Visual).

Discussing it with peers (Auditory) or applying it in a project (Kinesthetic) solidifies that knowledge, transforming it into a practical skill.

Example of blending learning styles

Let’s say you want to learn about web development.

You start by reading a book or online articles about HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (Reading/Writing). You then watch YouTube tutorials or online courses that visually demonstrate how these languages work together to build a website (Visual).

Finally, you put your knowledge to the test by creating your own website (Kinesthetic).

The combination of these resources leads to a more wholesome learning experience, allowing you to understand web development from different angles.

It also shows that while identifying your dominant learning style is beneficial, it is flexibility and the ability to adapt to different learning methods that are equally important on your path to mastering programming.

Personally, I like to blend reading with kinesthetic.

I always seek documentation first and prefer written step-by-step guides to watching videos.

I find videos slow me down and I just want to get to the building part!

Therefore, if I can read through a tutorial, I can go straight to the bits I find most useful, and even highlight and save them for later.

When I do watch videos, I prefer documentary or lecture styles for learning rather than ‘code-alongs’ - but hey ho that’s just me!

The best thing is that there are so many resources available, when you find what style you like, there will always likely be a resource to match it.

Conclusion

Each individual's path in programming is just as unique as the person.

Personalising your learning journey isn't just about choosing a programming language or deciding which project to build next.

It includes understanding how you learn most effectively and selecting resources that align with that style.

Understanding your learning style can drastically boost the efficiency of your learning process.

Rather than struggling through resources that don't align with your style, you can select materials that complement the way you naturally learn.

By doing so, you're setting yourself up for success, ensuring that you not only learn but genuinely understand and enjoy the process of learning how to code.

As you continue your journey in programming, try to explore different resources, try out various learning strategies, and discover what works best for you.

Don't be afraid to step out of your comfort zone - it's often where the most significant growth happens.

Take time to reflect on your learning experiences, adjusting your strategies as you learn more about yourself and the awesome land of programming.

Find your unique style!

From your fellow ever-growing dev,

Cherlock Code

Items.Title: 使用 Sigstore 保护您的 Kubernetes 工作负载 | Securing your Kubernetes workloads with Sigstore
Items.Description:
日期：2023-07-20

考虑您的典型 CI/CD 管道，如下所示。 下面与 DevOps 工作流程相关的问题有哪些（如果有）？

主要问题是安全措施没有作为一等公民纳入管道中。 我们假设最好的情况是托管生产工作负载的 Kubernetes 集群事后得到了合理的保护。 在这种情况下，试图破坏集群的恶意行为者可能会在无法访问节点本身的情况下尝试直接访问运行工作负载的 Pod，而不是以此为起点。 然而，这种攻击媒介并不是非常有效 - 由于 Pod 本身是短暂的并且不断被重新创建，因此攻击者可能很难维持对受感染工作负载的访问，更不用说在集群中导航了。

相反，攻击者可能会尝试直接从源头破坏生产工作负载，通过寻找方法将漏洞和/或恶意软件直接注入源代码本身（例如，首先获得对开发人员工作站或笔记本电脑的访问权限）或感染生成图像的构建环境 - 这通常称为软件供应链攻击。 因此，受损的应用程序映像作为部署到集群的 Kubernetes 部署进入生产环境 - 由于映像本身已经被感染，因此从该部署创建和重新创建的所有 Pod 都肯定会受到感染，从而减轻了攻击者维护对受感染工作负载的访问以及随后在集群中工作的负担。

为了防止像上面所示的软件供应链攻击，安全措施应该作为一等公民主动集成到管道中，DevOps 专业人员应该将安全实践集成到他们的日常职责中 - 这种将安全集成到 DevOps 的实践也称为 DevSecOps。

例如，请考虑以下具有内置自动漏洞扫描和图像签名的 DevSecOps 管道。 这个想法是，仅当漏洞扫描报告映像不包含恶意软件和/或高严重性漏洞时，映像才会被自动签名并部署到集群； 否则图像将被拒绝。 该集群还强制执行一项策略，仅将其映像包含适当签名的工作负载部署到集群，以防止攻击者绕过管道并尝试将未签名（可能受感染）的映像直接部署到集群。

在接下来的实验室中，我们将介绍一个专为 DevSecOps 工作流程设计、致力于提高软件供应链安全性的开源项目——Sigstore 。

继续阅读donaldsebleung.com

Date: 2023-07-20

Consider your typical CI/CD pipeline as shown below. What are some of the issues associated with the DevOps workflow below, if any?

The main issue is that security measures are not integrated into the pipeline as a first-class citizen. Let's assume the best case where the Kubernetes cluster hosting the production workloads is reasonably secured as an afterthought. In this case, a malicious actor seeking to compromise the cluster might, failing to gain access to the nodes themselves, attempt to gain access to the Pods running the workloads directly instead as a starting point. However, this vector of attack is not terribly effective - since Pods themselves are ephemeral and are constantly being recreated, the attacker would likely have a hard time maintaining access to the infected workload, let alone navigating their way within the cluster.

Instead, what an attacker might do is attempt to compromise the production workload(s) straight from the source, by finding ways to inject vulnerabilities and/or malware directly into the source code itself (e.g. by first gaining access to the developer's workstation or laptop) or infecting the build environment where images are generated - this is commonly known as a software supply chain attack. As a result, the compromised application image makes its way to production as a Kubernetes Deployment which is deployed to the cluster - since the image itself is already infected, all Pods created and re-created from that Deployment are guaranteed to be infected, easing the attacker's burden of maintaining access to that infected workload and subsequently working their way around the cluster.

To prevent software supply chain attacks like the one shown above, security measures should be proactively integrated into the pipeline as a first-class citizen and DevOps professionals should integrate security practices into their day-to-day duties - this practice of integrating security into DevOps is also known as DevSecOps.

For example, consider the following DevSecOps pipeline with automated vulnerability scanning and image signing built-in. The idea is that the image will be automatically signed and deployed to the cluster only if vulnerability scanning reports that the image does not contain malware and/or high-severity vulnerabilities; otherwise the image is rejected. The cluster also enforces a policy such that only workloads whose images contain the appropriate signature(s) can be deployed to the cluster, to prevent attackers from circumventing the pipeline and attempting to deploy unsigned (potentially infected) images directly to the cluster.

In the lab that follows, we will introduce an open source project designed for DevSecOps workflows and dedicated to improving software supply chain security - Sigstore.

Items.Title: 🗣️🤖 询问您的 NLP 中的 Neo4J 知识库并获取 KPI | 🗣️🤖 Ask to your Neo4J knowledge base in NLP & get KPIs
Items.Description:
阿德里安斯选择北卡罗来纳州

发表于 7 月 20 日

通过这些内容，我们将开始揭示一个有趣的主题，即：

问题/答案的成本/价值平衡……以及“无偿的幻觉”

正如您将看到的， AI （尤其是AGI ）引发了很多关于任务侵占的担忧……但也有助于挑战诸如成本与智能之间的比率、它对我们的协作已经产生的影响等深层次问题……最重要的是：

在这种情况下， NeoDash上实现了无代码集成：

这篇文章将会出现：

  • 在进一步讨论之前，我们需要了解我们要做的就是：

    1. 问一个NLP问题（目前是英文）
    2. 调用openai的LLM API来查询元图APOC并构建cypher查询
    3. 获取cypher （查询）
    4. 在本地针对数据运行密码
    5. 定制报告

    在您的日常活动中，您可能会遇到以下类型的问题（即遵循相同的模式） ：

    • 我们有多少GitHub 用户？ 我们有多少虚拟机？ 我们要花多少钱...
    • 下面放大了图拓扑，以便您（以及我们稍后将使用的LLM ）可以了解如何处理数据：

      现在我们可以将问题转换为仪表板小部件：

      随着第一次实验开始出现新的问题，特别是以下问题：

      本节致力于非常简要地总结这些问题，以便我们可以讨论它们并优化我们解决方案的设计、我们管理和回答传入问题的方式……以及我们自己的大部分学习路径，以便我们可以将自动化任务委托给人工智能和……

      重点关注那些不能或尚未自动化的领域。

      事实上，很快您就会愿意自动化回答问题的任务并重新投入时间。

      简而言之：

      • 无需技能
      • 即插即用策略
      • 在查询原型阶段可能会非常昂贵（但请记住， OpenAI在改进 API 的同时会定期降低其 API 的价格）
      • 证明“时间就是金钱”
      • 将数据人员从潜在的瓶颈情况中移除，人们期望他们以更快的速度产生更多的知识
      • 制作原型非常方便
      • 非常适合高水平问题，并且非技术人员在技术上也能负担得起（这让他们自由）
      • 无需了解知识图谱设计
      • 容易产生误解和cypher错误（注意大上下文，见下文）和足够的LLM模型设置

      以下是需要考虑的几点：

      • 实施所需的技能
      • 需要知道预设的问题
      • 当链无法找到答案时，可以退回到全局方法......只需抛出“对不起，但使用我当前的工具集，我无法找到合适的答案。”

      这就是现在的全部内容，希望您会发现这些内容很有用，并让您考虑围绕人工智能采用的新用例。

      最后但并非最不重要的一点是，阅读这本书也非常有助于以新的方式思考工作本身并为工作做好准备。 未来展示：

adriens for opt-nc

Posted on Jul 20

With this content we'll start to uncover a fascinating subject, which is:

The cost/value balance of a question/answer... and "the illusion of gratuitousness"

As you will see, AI (and especially AGI) throws a lot of worries about task encroachment... but also helps challenging deep questions about what is the ratio between cost & intelligence, the impact it does already have on our collaborations... and most important :

"How we join our intelligence and creativity into a hybrid Humain/AI taskforce?"

🗞️ Neo4j, LLMs and data


In this context no-code integration has landed on NeoDash:

This post will show up:

  • 🧐 Why it matters
  • 🎯 What can be achieved with this new feature (and its potential impact on our organizations & workflows)

🚼 How it works

Before going further, we need to understand that all that we are going to do is:

  1. Ask a question in NLP (in english for now)
  2. Call openai's LLM API to query meta graph APOC and build the cypher query
  3. Get the cypher (the query)
  4. Locally run the cypher against the data
  5. Customize reports

🤔 Questions

On your daily activities, you may be challenged by the following kind of questions that look like (ie. follow the same pattern) :

  • How many GitHub users do we have ? (as it involves costs)
  • Who are the people managed by Mr X ?
  • How many GitHub repositories does Team X maintain ? (involves capacity to maintain a set of services)
  • How many virtual machine do we have ? (involves size of infrastructures and then costs,...)
  • How much do we spend on...
  • etc...

👉 Therefore we built an omniscient knowledge graph that is able to answer a very wide amount of questions about our organization.

Below some zoom-in into graph topology, so you (as well as the LLM we will use later) can understand how to deal with data:

Now we are able to transform a question into a dashboard widget:

🍿 Demo

⚖️ Further on strategies

With that first experimentation started to appear new kind of concerns, in particular the following one:

"A big omniscient giant vs. a myriad of little agents equipped of custom tools."

This section is dedicated to very briefly summarize these questions so we can talk about them and optimize the design of our solutions, the way we could manage & answer incoming questions... and most of our own learning path so we can delegate automated tasks to AI and...

focus on the ones that can't or are not yet automated.

💰 Benefits

As a matter of fact, pretty shortly you're willing to automate questions answering tasks and re-invest that time.

In short:

  • 🔗‍💥 People don't wait answers from your part : they go faster with more freedom, they don't make issues, etc...
  • 🧑‍🚀 You can focus on higher value added activities : you go further and faster & gain in expertise

📊 NO-Code Neodash

  • No skill required
  • Plug & play strategy
  • Could be quite expensive during query prototyping phasis (but keep in mnd that OpenAI is regularly reducing the price of its APIs while improving them)
  • Put in evidence that "Time is money"
  • Remove the data guys from a potential bottleneck situations where people expect them to produce more knowledge at a faster pace
  • Very convenient to prototype
  • Great for high level questions and technically affordable by non-tech people (which sets them free)
  • No need to know the knowledge graph design
  • 100% AI driven and meta-schema driven
  • Prone to make misunderstandings and cypher error generation (beware of large contexts, see below) and adequate LLM model setup

🦜🔗 Langchain & custom tools

Langchain and the implementation of Custom Tools also is a great (and very efficient) way to setup a dedicated Q&A (for example for chat purpose) agent.

Here are some few points to consider:

  • Skills required for implementation
  • Requires to know a preset of questions
  • Could fall back to the global approach when the chain is not able to find the answser... simply throw "I'm sorry, but with my current toolset, I was not able to find a suitable answer."

That's all for now, hopefully you'll have found this content useful and made you consider new use cases around AI adoption.

📘 Book

Last but not least, reading this book also highly helped considerate new way of thinking the work itself and prepare the future present:

Items.Title: 在 Dart 中构建高效数据结构的综合指南 | A Comprehensive Guide for Building Efficient Data Structures in Dart
Items.Description:
瓦迪姆·平丘克

发表于 7 月 20 日

在本文中，我们将探讨主要的数据结构，例如树、哈希图、链表、队列、堆栈、堆和无向图。 我们讨论它们的功能、空间和时间复杂性，并提供 Dart 中的代码示例。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，这本综合指南都将为您提供在编程工作中有效利用数据结构的知识。

内容概述

  1. 介绍
  2. 队列
  3. 堆
  4. 单链表
  5. 双链表
  6. 哈希图（哈希表）
  7. 最小/最大堆
  8. 无向图
  9. 树木
  10. 树：二叉搜索树 (BST)
  11. 树：红黑树
  12. 结论

介绍

数据结构是计算机科学的基本构建模块，在有效组织和操作数据方面发挥着至关重要的作用。 无论您是学习编程的初学者还是希望刷新知识的经验丰富的开发人员，了解不同数据结构的工作原理都是至关重要的。 我们将深入数据结构的世界，探索它们的内部工作原理、空间和时间复杂性以及最佳用例。 我们将提供 Dart（一种多功能编程语言）的代码示例，以帮助您掌握概念并了解它们的实际效果。 读完本文后，您将对各种数据结构有一个扎实的了解，并准备好应用它们来解决实际问题。在 Dart 语言中，没有传统意义上称为“数组”的特定数据类型。 相反，List 类用作保存元素集合的基本数据结构。 它作为一个多功能容器，提供类似数组的功能和操作。

队列

队列是一种遵循先进先出 (FIFO) 原则的线性数据结构。 元素在后面添加并从前面删除。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：入队：O(1) - 将元素添加到队列中。

出队：O(1) - 从队列中删除一个元素。

使用案例：当需要遵循先进先出（FIFO）原则时使用，例如在任务调度系统或广度优先搜索算法中。

 class Queue<T> { List<T> _items = []; void enqueue(T item) { _items.add(item); } T dequeue() { if (isEmpty()) { throw StateError("Cannot dequeue from an empty queue."); } return _items.removeAt(0); } bool isEmpty() { return _items.isEmpty; } }

堆

堆栈是一种遵循后进先出 (LIFO) 原则的线性数据结构。 元素从同一端（称为顶部）添加和删除。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：压入：O(1) — 将元素添加到堆栈中。

用例：当需要遵循后进先出 (LIFO) 原则时使用，例如在函数调用堆栈或深度优先搜索算法中。

 class Stack<T> { List<T> _items = []; void push(T item) { _items.add(item); } T pop() { if (isEmpty()) { throw StateError("Cannot pop from an empty stack."); } return _items.removeLast(); } bool isEmpty() { return _items.isEmpty; } }

单链表

链表是线性数据结构，其中每个元素（节点）包含一个值和对下一个节点的引用。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：在开头插入： O(1) — 在链表开头添加新节点。 末尾插入：O(1) — 在链表末尾添加新节点。 删除：O(1) — 从链表中删除一个节点。

搜索：O(n)——在最坏的情况下，访问链表中的所有节点。

用例：需要在列表开头或结尾频繁插入和删除元素时使用

class Node<T> { T value; Node<T> next; Node(this.value); } class LinkedList<T> { Node<T> head; void insertAtBeginning(T value) { var newNode = Node<T>(value); newNode.next = head; head = newNode; } void insertAtEnd(T value) { var newNode = Node<T>(value); if (head == null) { head = newNode; } else { var current = head; while (current.next != null) { current = current.next; } current.next = newNode; } } void delete(T value) { if (head == null) { return; } if (head.value == value) { head = head.next; return; } var current = head; while (current.next != null) { if (current.next.value == value) { current.next = current.next.next; return; } current = current.next; } } }

双链表

双链表与单链表类似，但每个节点都有对下一个和前一个节点的引用。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：在开头插入： O(1) — 在链表开头添加新节点。 末尾插入：O(1) — 在链表末尾添加新节点。 删除：O(1) — 从链表中删除一个节点。

搜索：O(n)——在最坏的情况下，访问链表中的所有节点。

用例：需要在列表开头或结尾频繁插入和删除元素时使用

class Node<T> { T value; Node<T> next; Node<T> previous; Node(this.value); } class DoublyLinkedList<T> { Node<T> head; void insertAtBeginning(T value) { var newNode = Node<T>(value); if (head != null) { head.previous = newNode; } newNode.next = head; head = newNode; } void insertAtEnd(T value) { var newNode = Node<T>(value); if (head == null) { head = newNode; } else { var current = head; while (current.next != null) { current = current.next; } newNode.previous = current; current.next = newNode; } } void delete(T value) { if (head == null) { return; } if (head.value == value) { head = head.next; if (head != null) { head.previous = null; } return; } var current = head; while (current != null) { if (current.value == value) { current.previous.next = current.next; if (current.next != null) { current.next.previous = current.previous; } return; } current = current.next; } } }

哈希图（哈希表）

哈希映射将键值对存储在数组中，使用哈希函数来确定每个键值对应存储的索引。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：插入：O(1) — 将键值对添加到哈希映射。 删除：O(1) — 从哈希映射中删除键值对。

搜索：O(1) — 检索与给定键关联的值。

用例：当键和值之间的关系不一定是有序的时，用于基于键快速查找值。

 class HashMap<K, V> { int _capacity; List<List<Entry<K, V>>> _buckets; HashMap({int capacity = 16}) { _capacity = capacity; _buckets = List<List<Entry<K, V>>>.filled(_capacity, []); } int _hashCode(K key) { return key.hashCode % _capacity; } void put(K key, V value) { int index = _hashCode(key); List<Entry<K, V>> bucket = _buckets[index]; for (Entry<K, V> entry in bucket) { if (entry.key == key) { entry.value = value; return; } } bucket.add(Entry<K, V>(key, value)); } V get(K key) { int index = _hashCode(key); List<Entry<K, V>> bucket = _buckets[index]; for (Entry<K, V> entry in bucket) { if (entry.key == key) { return entry.value; } } return null; } void remove(K key) { int index = _hashCode(key); List<Entry<K, V>> bucket = _buckets[index]; for (Entry<K, V> entry in bucket) { if (entry.key == key) { bucket.remove(entry); return; } } } bool containsKey(K key) { int index = _hashCode(key); List<Entry<K, V>> bucket = _buckets[index]; for (Entry<K, V> entry in bucket) { if (entry.key == key) { return true; } } return false; } } class Entry<K, V> { final K key; V value; Entry(this.key, this.value); }

最小/最大堆

堆是基于二叉树的数据结构，其中父节点相对于其子节点具有特定的排序（例如，最小值或最大值）。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：插入：O(log n) — 将一个元素添加到堆中。 删除：O(log n) — 从堆中删除根元素。

用例：用于在恒定时间内高效检索最小或最大元素或对元素进行高效排序。

 class MinHeap { List<int> _heap; MinHeap() { _heap = []; } // Insert an element into the min heap void insert(int value) { // Add the new value to the end of the heap _heap.add(value); // Restore the min heap property // by moving the value up if necessary _moveUp(_heap.length - 1); } // Extract the minimum element from the heap int extractMin() { if (isEmpty()) { throw Exception("Heap is empty"); } // The minimum value is always at the root (index 0) final minValue = _heap[0]; // Remove the last element from the heap final lastValue = _heap.removeLast(); if (!isEmpty()) { // Move the last value to the root _heap[0] = lastValue; // Restore the min heap property // by moving the value down if necessary _moveDown(0); } return minValue; } bool isEmpty() { return _heap.isEmpty; } // Get the index of the parent node of a given index int _parentIndex(int index) { return (index - 1) ~/ 2; } // Get the index of the left child node of a given index int _leftChildIndex(int index) { return 2 * index + 1; } // Get the index of the right child node of a given index int _rightChildIndex(int index) { return 2 * index + 2; } // Move a value up the heap // to restore the min heap property void _moveUp(int index) { while (index > 0 && _heap[index] < _heap[_parentIndex(index)]) { final parentIndex = _parentIndex(index); _swap(index, parentIndex); index = parentIndex; } } // Move a value down the heap to restore the min heap property void _moveDown(int index) { while (true) { var smallest = index; final lChildIdx = _leftChildIndex(index); final rChildIdx = _rightChildIndex(index); if (lChildIdx < _heap.length && _heap[lChildIdx] < _heap[smallest]) { // Update the smallest index if the left child is smaller smallest = lChildIdx; } if (rChildIdx < _heap.length && _heap[rChildIdx] < _heap[smallest]) { // Update the smallest index if the right child is smaller smallest = rChildIdx; } if (smallest == index) { break; } // Swap the value with the smallest child _swap(index, smallest); // Move down to the smallest child index index = smallest; } } void _swap(int i, int j) { final temp = _heap[i]; _heap[i] = _heap[j]; _heap[j] = temp; } }

要将最大堆转换为最小堆，需要进行以下更改：

  • 更改比较运算符：在最大堆中，用于比较元素的比较运算符通常为“大于”(>)。 要将其转换为最小堆，您需要将比较运算符更改为“小于”(<) 或“小于或等于”(<=)。
  • 调整heapify操作：heapify操作负责维护堆属性。 在最大堆中，它确保最大元素位于根。 在最小堆中，需要对其进行修改以确保最小元素位于根部。
  • 更新依赖于最大堆假设的任何相关代码：如果代码中有任何其他部分假设最大堆，例如提取最大元素或基于最大堆属性的操作，您可能需要相应地修改它们以使用最小堆。

无向图

无向图由一组由边连接的顶点组成，其中边没有方向。 检查不同类型的图数据结构

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：添加顶点：O(1) — 向图中添加一个顶点。 添加边：O(1) — 在两个顶点之间添加一条边。 删除顶点：O(V + E) — 删除顶点和所有关联的边。

删除边：O(1) - 删除两个顶点之间的边。

用例：用于表示实体之间的关系或解决与图论相关的问题，例如寻路算法。

 class Graph { Map<String, List<String>> _adjacencyList = {}; void addVertex(String vertex) { if (!_adjacencyList.containsKey(vertex)) { _adjacencyList[vertex] = []; } } void addEdge(String vertex1, String vertex2) { _adjacencyList[vertex1]?.add(vertex2); _adjacencyList[vertex2]?.add(vertex1); } void removeVertex(String vertex) { _adjacencyList.remove(vertex); _adjacencyList.forEach((key, value) => value.remove(vertex)); } void removeEdge(String vertex1, String vertex2) { _adjacencyList[vertex1]?.remove(vertex2); _adjacencyList[vertex2]?.remove(vertex1); } }

树木

在计算机科学中，树是一种广泛使用的数据结构，表示层次结构。 就像现实生活中的树一样，它由通过边连接的节点组成。 树中的每个节点都可以有零个或多个子节点，但根节点除外，根节点没有父节点。 树中的节点以特定方式组织，以允许有效的数据操作和检索。

树木类型：

  • 二叉树：二叉树是一种树结构，其中每个节点最多有两个子节点，称为左子节点和右子节点。 二叉树通常用作更复杂的树结构的基础。
  • 二叉搜索树（BST）：二叉搜索树是一棵二叉树，其中节点的左子树中的值小于该节点的值，而右子树中的值大于或等于该节点的值。 它为每个节点维护一个平衡因子，确保左右子树的高度最多相差1。 红黑树：红黑树是另一种自平衡二叉搜索树。 它通过为节点分配颜色（红色或黑色）并在插入和删除操作期间执行旋转和颜色调整来确保平衡的属性。 红黑树为操作提供了高效的对数时间复杂度，并广泛应用于各种应用中。
  • 它允许每个节点有多个键和子指针，从而在外部存储系统中实现高效的数据存储和检索。
  • 它将字符串的字符存储在树状结构中，使其能够有效地进行基于前缀的搜索。

常见树应用：

  • 文件系统：操作系统中的目录结构通常使用树来表示。
  • 数据库系统：B 树等索引结构用于高效的数据检索。
  • 编译器设计：语法树用于表示程序的结构。
  • 人工智能：决策树和搜索树用于各种人工智能算法。
  • 网络：路由算法使用基于树的数据结构来实现高效的数据包路由。
  • 分层数据表示：树用于表示数据中的分层关系，例如组织结构图或家谱。

平衡树
平衡树是一种树数据结构，其中任何节点的子树的高度最多相差一个常数因子。 这种平衡确保树保持相对对称，并避免创建可能导致操作效率低下的长路径。 平衡树旨在为各种操作（例如搜索、插入和删除）提供最佳性能。

平衡树的一些示例包括 AVL 树、红黑树和 B 树。 这些树采用特定的平衡机制，例如旋转和颜色调整，以保持其平衡特性。

平衡树的好处：

  • 高效的操作：平衡树为搜索、插入和删除等操作提供高效的时间复杂度。 这些操作通常具有对数时间复杂度，即使树的大小增加也能确保快速访问元素。
  • 稳定的性能：通过保持平衡，这些树可以防止最坏的情况，即树变得高度倾斜并且性能显着下降。 平衡树在广泛的输入范围内提供稳定且可预测的性能。
  • 最佳空间利用率：平衡树通过最小化树的高度来优化空间利用率。 这确保了树结构占用合理的内存量，无论其包含的元素数量如何。

不平衡树
另一方面，不平衡的树不能保持平衡属性。 因此，节点的子树的高度可能会显着不同，从而导致树结构倾斜或拉长。

在不平衡树中，某些操作可能会变得低效。 例如，搜索元素可能需要遍历很长的路径，导致时间复杂度更接近线性而不是对数。 同样，插入或删除节点可能会破坏树的结构，从而降低后续操作的效率。

不平衡的树可能由于多种原因而发生，例如不正确的插入和删除操作或导致树变得不平衡的特定数据模式。

不平衡树木的影响：

  • 性能下降：不平衡的树可能会导致搜索、插入和删除等操作性能不佳。 时间复杂度可能不再是对数的，树的效率随着元素数量的增长而降低。
  • 增加内存消耗：在某些情况下，不平衡树可能需要比平衡树更多的内存来存储相同数量的元素。 这是因为不平衡的树可能有更长的路径和更高的高度。

值得注意的是，不平衡树本质上并不是在所有情况下都是不好或不合适的。 在某些特定情况下，不平衡树仍然可以提供可接受的性能，特别是当树相对较浅或元素数量较少时。

二叉搜索树 (BST)

二叉搜索树是一种基于二叉树的数据结构，其中左子节点的值小于其父节点，右子节点的值大于或等于其父节点。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：搜索：O(log n) — 在 BST 中搜索值。 插入：O(log n) — 将一个值插入到 BST 中。 删除：O(log n) — 从 BST 中删除一个值。

中序遍历：O(n) — 按升序访问所有节点。

用例：有效地搜索已排序集合中的元素。 按排序顺序维护元素集合。 高效执行范围查询（查找给定范围内的元素）。

通过高效的键值查找实现关联数组。


 class Node<T> { T value; Node<T> left; Node<T> right; Node(this.value); } class BinarySearchTree<T extends Comparable<T>> { Node<T> _root; void insert(T value) { _root = _insertRecursive(_root, value); } Node<T> _insertRecursive(Node<T> node, T value) { if (node == null) { return Node<T>(value); } if (value.compareTo(node.value) < 0) { node.left = _insertRecursive(node.left, value); } else { node.right = _insertRecursive(node.right, value); } return node; } bool search(T value) { return _searchRecursive(_root, value); } bool _searchRecursive(Node<T> node, T value) { if (node == null) { return false; } if (value.compareTo(node.value) == 0) { return true; } if (value.compareTo(node.value) < 0) { return _searchRecursive(node.left, value); } else { return _searchRecursive(node.right, value); } } void delete(T value) { _root = _deleteRecursive(_root, value); } Node<T> _deleteRecursive(Node<T> node, T value) { if (node == null) { return node; } if (value.compareTo(node.value) < 0) { node.left = _deleteRecursive(node.left, value); } else if (value.compareTo(node.value) > 0) { node.right = _deleteRecursive(node.right, value); } else { if (node.left == null && node.right == null) { node = null; } else if (node.left == null) { node = node.right; } else if (node.right == null) { node = node.left; } else { var minValue = _findMinValue(node.right); node.value = minValue; node.right = _deleteRecursive(node.right, minValue); } } return node; } T _findMinValue(Node<T> node) { while (node.left != null) { node = node.left; } return node.value; } void inOrderTraversal() { _inOrderTraversalRecursive(_root); } void _inOrderTraversalRecursive(Node<T> node) { if (node != null) { _inOrderTraversalRecursive(node.left); print(node.value); _inOrderTraversalRecursive(node.right); } } }

红黑树

红黑树是一种自平衡二叉搜索树，它通过在节点上强制执行某些属性（例如着色）来确保树保持平衡。

空间复杂度：
时间复杂度：搜索：O(log n) — 在红黑树中搜索一个值。 插入：O(log n) — 将一个值插入到红黑树中。 删除：O(log n) — 从红黑树中删除一个值。

中序遍历：O(n) — 按升序访问所有节点。

用例：高效维护有序集合，同时支持高效的插入和删除操作。 实现有序数据结构，例如区间树或增强搜索树。 高效搜索、插入和删除至关重要的数据库索引和存储系统。 枚举 NodeColor { 红，黑 }

 class RBNode<T extends Comparable<T>> { T value; RBNode<T> left; RBNode<T> right; RBNode<T> parent; NodeColor color; RBNode(this.value) { color = NodeColor.red; } } class RedBlackTree<T extends Comparable<T>> { RBNode<T> _root; void insert(T value) { var newNode = RBNode<T>(value); _insertNode(newNode); _fixViolation(newNode); } void _insertNode(RBNode<T> newNode) { if (_root == null) { _root = newNode; _root.color = NodeColor.black; return; } var current = _root; RBNode<T> parent; while (current != null) { parent = current; if (newNode.value.compareTo(current.value) < 0) { current = current.left; } else { current = current.right; } } newNode.parent = parent; if (newNode.value.compareTo(parent.value) < 0) { parent.left = newNode; } else { parent.right = newNode; } } void _fixViolation(RBNode<T> node) { RBNode<T> parent; RBNode<T> grandparent; while (node != _root && node.color == NodeColor.red && node.parent.color == NodeColor.red) { parent = node.parent; grandparent = parent.parent; if (parent == grandparent.left) { var uncle = grandparent.right; if (uncle != null && uncle.color == NodeColor.red) { parent.color = NodeColor.black; uncle.color = NodeColor.black; grandparent.color = NodeColor.red; node = grandparent; } else { if (node == parent.right) { _rotateLeft(parent); node = parent; parent = node.parent; } _rotateRight(grandparent); var temp = parent.color; parent.color = grandparent.color; grandparent.color = temp; node = parent; } } else { var uncle = grandparent.left; if (uncle != null && uncle.color == NodeColor.red) { parent.color = NodeColor.black; uncle.color = NodeColor.black; grandparent.color = NodeColor.red; node = grandparent; } else { if (node == parent.left) { _rotateRight(parent); node = parent; parent = node.parent; } _rotateLeft(grandparent); var temp = parent.color; parent.color = grandparent.color; grandparent.color = temp; node = parent; } } } _root.color = NodeColor.black; } void _rotateLeft(RBNode<T> node) { var rightChild = node.right; node.right = rightChild.left; if (rightChild.left != null) { rightChild.left.parent = node; } rightChild.parent = node.parent; if (node.parent == null) { _root = rightChild; } else if (node == node.parent.left) { node.parent.left = rightChild; } else { node.parent.right = rightChild; } rightChild.left = node; node.parent = rightChild; } void _rotateRight(RBNode<T> node) { var leftChild = node.left; node.left = leftChild.right; if (leftChild.right != null) { leftChild.right.parent = node; } leftChild.parent = node.parent; if (node.parent == null) { _root = leftChild; } else if (node == node.parent.right) { node.parent.right = leftChild; } else { node.parent.left = leftChild; } leftChild.right = node; node.parent = leftChild; } bool search(T value) { var current = _root; while (current != null) { if (value.compareTo(current.value) == 0) { return true; } else if (value.compareTo(current.value) < 0) { current = current.left; } else { current = current.right; } } return false; } }

结论

在本文中，我们探索了各种数据结构及其在 Dart 中的实现。 我们涵盖了各种基本数据结构，包括集合、队列、链表、双向链表（deques）、哈希图、无向图、堆、二叉搜索树（BST）和红黑树。

集合允许有效地存储唯一元素并执行集合操作，例如并集、交集和差集。 队列和双端队列用于以先进先出（FIFO）方式管理元素，双端队列还允许在两端进行高效的插入和删除。

链表为动态元素插入和删除提供了灵活性，而哈希映射则可以基于哈希函数实现快速键值查找。 引入无向图作为表示实体之间关系的一种方式，并且我们探索了不同的图遍历算法。 堆，特别是最小堆和最大堆变体，被讨论为树状结构，可用于维护根处的最小或最大元素。

最后，我们深入研究了二叉搜索树（BST）和红黑树，它们是自平衡二叉搜索树。 我们研究了它们的属性、插入、删除和搜索等操作，以及它们在有效维护有序集合方面的优势。

通过涵盖这些不同的数据结构，读者可以全面了解其功能、性能特征和最佳用例。 有了这些知识，开发人员就可以针对其特定问题领域选择和实施最合适的数据结构，优化效率并实现可扩展的解决方案。

In this article, we explore the main data structures such as trees, hash maps, linked lists, queues, stacks, heaps, and undirected graphs. We discuss their functionalities, space and time complexities, and provide code examples in Dart. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this comprehensive guide will equip you with the knowledge to effectively utilise data structures in your programming endeavors.
